scores.correctness_notes: The response correctly explains oci-image resource declaration fields (type, description, upstream-source) but critically omits the containers section and the key rule that the resource name must match the `resource` field in the corresponding containers entry — a central requirement per the gold standard. Also incorrectly marks `description` as required when it is optional.
scores.hallucination_notes: description is marked as required (✅) when the gold standard specifies it is optional — minor factual inaccuracy. The charm code example uses `self.model.resources.fetch()` which is a real Ops API, not fabricated. No fully invented APIs.
